title: Update Contact Fields
description: Add the ability to update individual fields of existing contacts. Create update_contact() that finds a contact by name and updates specified fields using keyword arguments.
starter_code:
# Contact Manager — Step 7: Update Contacts
# Use assignments and dictionary updates to modify contact data.

contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Charlie', 'phone': '555-0003', 'email': 'charlie@example.com'},
]

def find_contact(contact_list, search_name):
    return [c for c in contact_list if search_name.lower() in c['name'].lower()]

def update_contact(contact_list, contact_name, **updates):
    """Find a contact by exact name (case-insensitive) and update fields.
    
    Example: update_contact(contacts, 'Alice', phone='555-9999')
    Returns True if contact was found and updated, False otherwise.
    Only updates fields that are valid (name, phone, email).
    """
    # Your code here
    return False

# Test it
print(f"Before: {contacts[0]}")
result = update_contact(contacts, 'Alice', phone='555-9999', email='newalice@example.com')
print(f"Updated: {result}")
print(f"After: {contacts[0]}")
test_code:
test_contacts = [
    {'name': 'Alice', 'phone': '555-0001', 'email': 'alice@example.com'},
    {'name': 'Bob', 'phone': '555-0002', 'email': 'bob@example.com'},
]
result = update_contact(test_contacts, 'Alice', phone='555-9999')
assert result is True, 'update_contact should return True when contact is found'
assert test_contacts[0]['phone'] == '555-9999', f"Phone should be updated to 555-9999, got {test_contacts[0]['phone']}"
assert test_contacts[0]['email'] == 'alice@example.com', 'Email should not change when only phone is updated'
result2 = update_contact(test_contacts, 'Bob', email='newbob@test.com', name='Bobby')
assert result2 is True, 'Should update multiple fields at once'
assert test_contacts[1]['name'] == 'Bobby', f"Name should be Bobby, got {test_contacts[1]['name']}"
assert test_contacts[1]['email'] == 'newbob@test.com', 'Email should be updated'
result3 = update_contact(test_contacts, 'alice', phone='555-0000')
assert result3 is True, 'Search should be case-insensitive'
result4 = update_contact(test_contacts, 'Nobody', phone='555-0000')
assert result4 is False, 'Should return False when contact not found'
result5 = update_contact(test_contacts, 'Alice', invalid_field='test')
assert 'invalid_field' not in test_contacts[0], 'Should not add invalid fields to contact'
acceptance_criteria: update_contact() finds contact by name (case-insensitive exact match)
Updates only the specified fields using **kwargs
Only allows valid fields (name, phone, email) — ignores unknown fields
Returns True if found and updated, False if not found
Modifies the contact in-place in the list
hints: Use **updates to accept any keyword arguments: def update_contact(contact_list, contact_name, **updates)
Loop through the list to find the contact whose name matches contact_name (case-insensitive)
Filter updates to valid keys: valid = {k: v for k, v in updates.items() if k in ('name', 'phone', 'email')}
Use contact.update(valid) to apply the changes
